title: Feature Request: Function to see at the Status LED if the Host does have an Authorized DNS Entry
author: Chrisu02
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

## Expected Behavior
Status RED : Host is not Reachable.
Status Amber: Host is reachable but only with unauthorized adress (nslookup)
Satus Green: Hostname has an Authorized DNS entry

## Current Behavior
Red: Hostname is not available
Green: Hostname is available

## Possible Solution
Adding an Option to add the FQDN domain as Option Field to the settings.
Function to Query the ping if it does have an "Authorized ...

iterations.description: Feature request for DNS authorization status LED indicator is still conceptual with unresolved technical approach (ICMP ping may be blocked, nslookup behavior unclear) and needs confirmation it's still desired on current fork builds.

comments.author: Chrisu02
comments.snippet:
Hello, thanks for thinking about it :)

I noticed that i missunderstood the code, its not the FQDN that is queried for authorized, for me all anwers ar unauthorized.
Its checking the nslookup entry.

so basicly it does ping the Host by its computername, then it does use the ip adress that has been resolved by the ping and does do a nslookup of this IP.

Then it does compare the output computername of the ip nslookup with the originally pinged computername,
If they do match, the Icon is g...

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: yes i am god on networking i am undestood yours idea :) but in many companies you should know ping could be disabled (for securyti reasons they say) so we cant trust it 100% but as option we may try to add such.

comments.author: robertpopa22
